entities.name: STREPTOMYCES SPP.
entities.description: A genus of bacteria evaluated as a potential biocontrol agent for plant growth-promotion and disease control.

entities.name: MACROPHOMINA PHASEOLINA
entities.description: The fungal causal agent of charcoal rot disease in sorghum.

entities.name: SORGHUM
entities.description: A cereal crop affected by charcoal rot and used to test the efficacy of Streptomyces strains.

entities.name: CHICKPEA
entities.description: A legume crop mentioned as a recipient of plant growth-promotion by Streptomyces strains.

entities.name: CHARCOAL ROT
entities.description: A disease of sorghum caused by the fungus Macrophomina phaseolina.

entities.name: DUAL CULTURE ASSAY
entities.description: A laboratory technique used to evaluate the antagonistic potential of Streptomyces strains against M. phaseolina.

entities.name: SECONDARY METABOLITE PRODUCTION ASSAY
entities.description: A laboratory technique used to evaluate the inhibition of M. phaseolina by metabolites produced by Streptomyces strains.

entities.name: GREENHOUSE DISEASE SCREEN
entities.description: An experimental setup used to evaluate the antifungal activity of Streptomyces strains on sorghum plants.

entities.name: TOOTH PICK METHOD
entities.description: A specific inoculation technique used within the greenhouse disease screen to test antifungal activity.

entities.name: SCANNING ELECTRON MICROSCOPE
entities.description: An imaging technique used to analyze the integrity of xylem and phloem tissues in sorghum stems.

entities.name: PLANT GROWTH-PROMOTION
entities.description: The ability of certain microorganisms to enhance the growth and development of plants.

entities.name: ANTIFUNGAL ACTIVITY
entities.description: The ability of a substance or organism to inhibit the growth of or destroy fungi.

entities.name: BIOCONTROL AGENT
entities.description: A biological organism used to suppress the population of a pest or pathogen.
